## Task
Found 2026-07-10 while closing STORE-002: rebuild reconstructs terminal states from a `closed: done|dropped` line in the packet export body (rebuild.ts parseTerminalStatus), but NOTHING writes that line — task close and the dropped path do not stamp the export. Only 18 of ~69 done packets carry the marker (historic hand-stamps). Consequence: the git floor (definitions + terminal states, the whole point of the exports) is broken for the other ~51; any disaster recovery depends 100% on .svp backups, and a rebuild reconstructs done work as draft (the new swap-guard refuses the swap, so no loss — but rebuild is then useless as a recovery path). Close the class:
1. task close (review->done, PR verified) and the drop path stamp `closed: done` / `closed: dropped` into the export body and regenerate the .md — same single export writer as amend, no second serializer.
2. One-time repair command or flag (e.g. `board sync --stamp-terminal`) that walks the live DB and stamps every terminal packet's export missing the marker — run once, commit the diff, and the git floor is whole again.
3. Doctor drift check: terminal packets in the DB whose export lacks the marker are reported (count + ids) — the gap can never silently reopen.
4. Round-trip test: close a packet, rebuild from exports in a fixture, assert the packet comes back done.

## RED test (write first)
In a close/export test add a test named exactly: "closing a packet stamps the terminal state into its export so rebuild round-trips it". Close a fixture packet with a verified-merged PR stub, assert the export gains the closed marker, then rebuild from exports and assert status done. Today close does not touch the export -> it FAILS.
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the test name "closing a packet stamps the terminal state into its export so rebuild round-trips it".

## Reuse
The export writer used by amend (single source); parseTerminalStatus in rebuild.ts (do not duplicate the marker format — export it as a shared constant); doctor's readout builders.

## Stop conditions
A second markdown serializer; stamping non-terminal states into exports; changing the marker format without updating rebuild's parser and the shared constant together; touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
